FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Class Expander

Namespace
FluentGpu.Controls
Assembly
FluentGpu.Controls.dll

A WinUI-flavoured Expander: a clickable header row with a trailing chevron over a collapsible content panel. The header toggles local Component state (or a controlled IsExpanded signal); the single chevron glyph is ROTATED by the computed ExpanderTemplateSettings (down collapsed → up expanded).

MOTION — a DELIBERATE divergence from WinUI: the card height EASES (WinUI's ExpandDown/CollapseUp storyboards SNAP the layout space and only translate the content, Expander.xaml:39-90), because FluentGpu's design goal is smooth transitions of content, not discrete layout repositions. The storyboard TIMINGS and EASINGS stay WinUI-exact: expand 333ms KeySpline(0,0,0,1), collapse 167ms KeySpline(1,1,0,1). The whole open/close is one declarative engine transition — Reflow on the content clip wrapper (the interpolated height runs through real layout each tick, so neighbouring content reflows smoothly and RIGIDLY), with Trailing riding the content's bottom edge on the reveal edge (the WinUI slide-out-from-under-the-header look, rounded bottom corners visible mid-motion). No control-local ticker, no per-frame re-render: the engine owns the motion.

CUSTOMIZATION goes through Parts (the one generic door — no per-feature knobs): every named template part accepts arbitrary element props, e.g. a sticky pinned header ([PartHeader] = b => b with { StickyTop = 8f, OnPinned = …, Fill = stuck.Value ? … : b.Fill }) or an edge-to-edge content panel ([PartContent] = c => c with { Padding = Edges4.All(0) }). Mechanics-critical props are re-asserted after the modifier, so customization can restyle everything but break nothing.

public sealed class Expander : Component

Fields

Content 

public Element Content

Field Value

Element

Header 

public string Header

Field Value

string

HeaderContent

Arbitrary header content (WinUI Expander.Header is object content, not just a string). When set it replaces the default header label; the chevron button stays. The element is given Grow = 1's slot in the header row, so a column of title + caption lays out naturally.

public Element? HeaderContent

Field Value

Element

InitiallyExpanded 

public bool InitiallyExpanded

Field Value

bool

IsExpanded

Optional CONTROLLED open state (the WinUI IsExpanded dependency property, two-way): when set, the expander reads this signal instead of its local state — writes from anywhere (an "expand all" button, a view-model) open/close it with the full motion — and the header click writes back into it.

public Signal<bool>? IsExpanded

Field Value

Signal<bool>

PartChevron

The trailing 32×32 chevron button (WinUI ExpanderChevron). Owned: OnRealized (rotation-tween ref, chained with any modifier-supplied handler).

public const string PartChevron = "Chevron"

Field Value

string

PartClip

The always-mounted reveal wrapper (WinUI ExpanderContentClip) — the SizeMode.Reflow host. Owned: ClipToBounds, Height (the open/closed toggle), Animate (the reflow spec), Children, OnRealized (chained). NOTE: also transform-owned mid-motion (Trailing anchor) — do not add StickyTop/TransformBind here.

public const string PartClip = "Clip"

Field Value

string

PartContent

The padded content panel (WinUI ExpanderContent). Owned: Children (the Content slot — restructure via the slot, restyle via this part: padding, fill, border, corners…).

public const string PartContent = "Content"

Field Value

string

PartHeader

The clickable header row (WinUI ExpanderHeader). Owned: OnClick (toggle), Role.

public const string PartHeader = "Header"

Field Value

string

PartRoot

The returned card root (pure-layout column). Owned: Children.

public const string PartRoot = "Root"

Field Value

string

Parts

Lightweight per-part styling (CSS ::part): modifiers keyed by the PartXxx consts; see the class remarks and TemplateParts for the contract.

public TemplateParts? Parts

FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
